author: @edstrrrr
text: 1:53 I think people forget Dave Filoni is good at making something that isn't necessarily amazing but isn't completely terrible and then improving it as it goes along look at clone wars and rebels both had rocky starts but are considered to be very enjoyable as they go along Ahsoka's first season wasn't the best but I would like to think Dave Filoni will do what he does and improve the second season.

replies.author: @Fardawg
replies.text: Dave was not the only person involved in making Clone Wars. George oversaw everything, giving them ideas on what stories he wanted to see and approving of every major decision since he saw the series as being in the same canon as his films. And then you have all the writers, producers, and directors other than Dave that worked on it. We have seen what Dave does when he has major control over a project, and that is Ahsoka, which sucked. He can't even make Thrawn a proper intimidating villain and he had Rebels to work that out on and failed there as well. Without Lucas and/or other creative producers, Dave makes crap.

replies.author: @takeo_624
replies.text: ​@FardawgThrawn was a great villain in Rebels and the series itself is amazing, Bad Batch is amazing, Tales are more hits than misses and saying that Ahsoka sucked when we got BoBF and Acolyte is laughable, not to mention Mandalorian that was his and Favreau's but tanked immediately after he left to work on other things

author: @veeclash4157
text: In Ahsoka Season 2, Sabine comes out as gay or nonbinary. Her whole sexual identity becomes the focus of the show and explains why she rejected getting together with Ezra in Season 1. This is totally something LucasFilms/Disney would do.

replies.author: @byallon
replies.text: Hmm Filoni still has the card of "these two are siblings"

replies.author: @veeclash4157
replies.text: @byallon Ezra and Sabine aren't siblings. One is Mandalorian. It felt like a cheap trick to have Sabine watch a holo of Ezra at the end of each day only to remove any emotional depth to the reunion when she finally found him.

replies.author: @J7nator
replies.text: Oh Fck

replies.author: @takeo_624
replies.text: If you genuinely believe that anything you wrote can happen then you never actually watched anything Filoni has put out, even your understanding of the Ahsoka finale is jarring because she didn't reject getting with Erza in season 1, if anything he sibling zoned her first and when she finally found him hell broke lose before they could talk anything out and she decided to stay in the new galaxy with Ahsoka so that her master wouldn't be left alone knowing that Erza was going back to warn the New Republic about Thrawn. Sabine actually made a selfless choice for the good of the entire galaxy AFTER having fucked up and unleashed one of the biggest threats the galaxy had ever seen because she wanted to see Erza so much, her character arc in season 1 was more thought out than anything in the sequels which isn't saying a lot but she also wasn't even the main character of the series

author: @schizer
text: 13:28 no I will disagree with you on this theory. I love you and what you do but your insistence on Star Wars being exclusively about the force is something I strongly disagree on. If what you say is true, then you will have to completely disregard the x wing squadron stories, the Han Solo trilogy, the Death Star novel etc. yes the force is still a huge part of the universe, but for you to dismiss a great in-universe story just because it doesn’t have lightsabers and the force is really puzzling for me

author: @SrLopez-do7rh
text: 3:40 Thats something DC is also doing now. James Gunn (creative side) and Peter Safran (Bureocratic side) came together (trusting each other) to try to make a great long term plan for the DC Universe and stick to it. And its been really nice

replies.author: @Smileydoge1234
replies.text: Eh. I’m not really thrilled with what they’re doing with the DCU. I know not everyone’s going to agree with me, and that’s fine, but everything released so far has been either mediocre or bad. Creature Commandos was the same story told again. Peacemaker Season 2 was disappointing. And while I do like David Corenswet’s Superman, I wasn’t a fan of the movie. I thought it was a case of a good Superman in a mediocre story, kinda like Andrew Garfield’s Spider Man. And Supergirl just looks like more Superhero slop with retro 90’s music and Marvel dialogue, aka a trope that’s been really overdone and tired of. It’s just a Guardians clone but everytime I bring it up some Stan is going to bring up how comic accurate it is and that I’m being a “bigot” just because it’s a woman, even though Ive said the same thing about Blue Beetle and Shazam. It all depends on the quality of the movie, but I don’t think James Gunns style of filmmaking is what DC (or even the entire Superhero genre) needs right now. That’s just my opinion tho.

replies.author: @SrLopez-do7rh
replies.text: ​@Smileydoge1234Understable. At least there is a long term plan and there trying to stick with it. That should be the case for all comic book franchices. And it wasn't for DC for a long time

author: @ShibuwuF7

Didn’t the asohka show end with the Thrawn “long live the empire” scene on the bridge?

author: @ThalionMoss
text: I think of Star Wars fandom as a huge stadium. For decades, a lot of people had seats with a perfect view of the field—the stories were made primarily for them. Now, the stadium has expanded. New sections were built for new audiences. Some people in the old seats don't like their new sightlines, and they're angry the stadium changed at all. Others are thrilled to finally be inside. The management changing one executive isn't going to demolish the new sections. The fix isn't about going back; it's about making sure every section gets a good show sometimes. You might just be in a section that's not being catered to right now."

I think it’s fair to assess everything in production right now and going forward on him!
